title: COPD triple therapy — strong and partial support
notes: Synthetic-but-realistic; controlled ground truth. Uses superscript-range citation style in the manuscript. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: Triple therapy reduced the annual rate of moderate-to-severe exacerbations compared with dual bronchodilator therapy.1 Triple therapy improved lung function and reduced all-cause mortality.1,2 Background therapy was standardised across groups.

goldClaims.originalText: Triple therapy reduced the annual rate of moderate-to-severe exacerbations compared with dual bronchodilator therapy.1
goldClaims.text: Triple therapy reduced the annual rate of moderate-to-severe exacerbations compared with dual bronchodilator therapy

goldClaims.originalText: Triple therapy improved lung function and reduced all-cause mortality.1,2
goldClaims.text: Triple therapy improved lung function and reduced all-cause mortality

goldNonClaims: Background therapy was standardised across groups.

1.name: IMPACT-style primary (Adeyemi 2023)
1.text: Triple therapy significantly reduced the annual rate of moderate-to-severe exacerbations compared with dual bronchodilator therapy (rate ratio 0.75; 95% CI 0.70-0.81; p<0.001). Trough FEV1 was significantly improved with triple therapy versus dual therapy (least-squares mean difference 97 mL; p<0.001).

2.name: Mortality sub-analysis
2.text: All-cause mortality was numerically lower with triple therapy, but the analysis was not powered for mortality and the difference did not reach statistical significance.

goldVerdicts.claimText: Triple therapy reduced the annual rate of moderate-to-severe exacerbations compared with dual bronchodilator therapy
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref states the exacerbation rate reduction explicitly with rate ratio and p<0.001.

goldVerdicts.claimText: Triple therapy improved lung function and reduced all-cause mortality
goldVerdicts.rationale: Lung-function improvement is explicit; mortality reduction is not established in ref 1 — compound claim, one half holds (rule 4).
